expectations.text: Mentions aerobic capacity testing (e.g., 2-mile run, 5K, or similar endurance event)
expectations.evidence: Section 4.1 covers '5K Run Time' and Section 4.2 covers '1-Mile Run Time' as cardiovascular endurance tests.

expectations.text: Mentions anaerobic or sprint testing (e.g., 400m, 800m, 40m dash, or 100m)
expectations.evidence: No specific sprint running tests (400m, 800m, 40m, 100m) are mentioned. Section 6.1 covers 'Assault Bike — 1-Minute Max Effort' which is anaerobic but not sprint running. The expected output specifically mentions running-based anaerobic tests.

expectations.text: Mentions explosive power testing (e.g., vertical jump, broad jump, or medicine ball throws)
expectations.evidence: Section 2.1 covers 'Vertical Jump', Section 2.2 covers 'Broad Jump', and Section 2.3 covers 'Medicine Ball Chest Pass (or Overhead Throw)' — all three expected tests.

expectations.text: Mentions strength testing (e.g., squat/bench/deadlift max or rep max)
expectations.evidence: Sections 1.1-1.4 cover Barbell Back Squat 1RM/3RM, Barbell Deadlift 1RM/3RM, Barbell Bench Press 1RM/3RM, and Weighted Pull-Up 1RM/3RM.

expectations.text: Acknowledges this full battery may be better suited to longer intervals (e.g., annual) rather than every 12 weeks
expectations.evidence: The answer recommends repeating tests every 12 weeks throughout. No mention that the full battery might be better as an annual or longer-interval assessment.
